title: Cyclosorus sumatranus
taxonomy: Cyclosorus sumatranus is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the kingdom Plantae. The classification of Cyclosorus sumatranus is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, division - Pteridophyta, class - Polypodiopsida, order - Polypodiales, family - Thelypteridaceae, genus - Cyclosorus, species - C. sumatranus. Cyclosorus sumatranus is a member of the genus Cyclosorus, which is a group of ferns that are native to tropical regions. The classification of Cyclosorus sumatranus is constantly being revised as new research emerges.
uses: Cyclosorus sumatranus, also known as the Sumatran cinnamon fern, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions. In traditional medicine, the fronds of the fern are used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant's extract is also reported to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it a potential candidate for wound healing and infection prevention. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Cyclosorus sumatranus for medicinal purposes.

constituents: Cyclosorus sumatranus, a species of fern, contains various active constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. preparations: Cyclosorus sumatranus is a type of fern that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asian countries. The plant's herbal preparations are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. intro: Cyclosorus sumatranus, also known as the Sumatran cyclosorus or Sumatran maiden's hair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus sumatranus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cyclosorus sumatranus is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to treat fever, sore throats, and digestive problems.
